_meta.purpose: PROPOSED page template for one step of a transactional purchase/booking flow. Which section patterns are required / optional / forbidden, and the order rules. NOT yet established — see PROPOSAL.md. Compose by SELECTING sections per content (sections.json → whenToUse) and ordering them per this template.
_meta.source: TO CAPTURE — derive from a real Figma checkout reference screen (record its node id).

progress.note: Exactly one step indicator, always the first section after the Header. Read-only. Blocked on the Stepper component gap (PROPOSAL.md #1).

notice.note: Optional single Notification for step-level info/errors. Drop when no message.

body.note: Choose body sections by mapping each content group to the best-fit pattern via sections.json → whenToUse: fare choice → option-list; data entry → form-fieldset; review → summary. A step that enters data alongside a live summary uses a two-column layout (form + summary aside) — confirm the Grid layout against the captured reference.

action-bar.note: Exactly one action bar, always last: secondary 'Zurück' + single Brand forward action.

rules: Header is always the first child (logo = home; no 'Startseite' nav item; meta-nav/action icons off unless a real action exists).
Progress/stepper is read-only and always first; it never carries a Button.

Action hierarchy: exactly ONE Brand button per step (the forward/pay action); 'Zurück' is a ghost/outlined secondary in the same button group; fare options are a Radio group (equal-item-action), never one Brand button per option; summary edit affordances are Links.
Every body section MUST carry a title (h2); inner headings (fieldset subheadings, option titles) are h3/h4.
Spacing 'small' throughout for a dense, form-focused layout; one consistent centered contentWidth.
Backgrounds: first section color.background.canvas, then alternate surface/canvas (zebra).
NEVER emit empty placeholders (empty Select 'Input Text', glyphless icons, ✕ icon buttons). Only add a field/Select/Icon/Button once its label/value/glyph/action is provided.
A clickable option card has exactly one interactive element (the Radio) — no nested button/link inside it.

Arrangement (layout-guidelines.md → 'Reihenfolgen'): equal/comparable content is arranged HORIZONTALLY (fare options as equal-height cards; short field pairs like Vorname/Nachname, PLZ/Ort), sequential/prioritized content VERTICALLY (fieldsets, section order). Combine both only when each group's reading order stays unambiguous; fall back to vertical responsively when width is tight.

Grouping (gestaltgesetze.md): keep equal elements identically styled (Ähnlichkeit); place related elements close and separate groups via cards/surfaces (Nähe, gemeinsame Regionen); make exactly one element the most prominent — the Brand forward action (Prägnanz).
